Vec<NodeOrString>, can_gc: CanGc) -> ErrorResult {\n        self.upcast::<Node>().replace_children(nodes, can_gc)\n    }\n\n    /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-parentnode-queryselector>\n    fn QuerySelector(&self, selectors: DOMString) -> Fallible<Option<DomRoot<Element>>> {\n        let root = self.upcast::<Node>();\n        root.query_selector(selectors)\n    }\n\n    /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-parentnode-queryselectorall>\n    fn QuerySelectorAll(&self, selectors: DOMString) -> Fallible<DomRoot<NodeList>> {\n        let root = self.upcast::<Node>();\n        root.query_selector_all(selectors)\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-readystate>\n    fn ReadyState(&self) -> DocumentReadyState {\n        self.ready_state.get()\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-defaultview>\n    fn GetDefaultView(&self) -> Option<DomRoot<Window>> {\n        if self.has_browsing_context {\n            Some(DomRoot::from_ref(&*self.window))\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-cookie>\n    fn GetCookie(&self) -> Fallible<DOMString> {\n        if self.is_cookie_averse() {\n            return Ok(DOMString::new());\n        }\n\n        if !self.origin.is_tuple() {\n            return Err(Error::Security(None));\n        }\n\n        let url = self.url();\n        let (tx, rx) = profile_ipc::channel(self.global().time_profiler_chan().clone()).unwrap();\n        let _ = self\n            .window\n            .as_global_scope()\n            .resource_threads()\n            .send(GetCookiesForUrl(url, tx, NonHTTP));\n        let cookies = rx.recv().unwrap();\n        Ok(cookies.map_or(DOMString::new(), DOMString::from))\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-cookie>\n    fn SetCookie(&self, cookie: DOMString) -> ErrorResult {\n        if self.is_cookie_averse() {\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n\n        if !self.origin.is_tuple() {\n            return Err(Error::Security(None));\n        }\n\n        if !cookie.is_valid_for_cookie() {\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n\n        let cookies = if let Some(cookie) = Cookie::parse(cookie.to_string()).ok().map(Serde) {\n            vec![cookie]\n        } else {\n            vec![]\n        };\n\n        let _ = self\n            .window\n            .as_global_scope()\n            .resource_threads()\n            .send(SetCookiesForUrl(self.url(), cookies, NonHTTP));\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-bgcolor>\n    fn BgColor(&self) -> DOMString {\n        self.get_body_attribute(&local_name!(\"bgcolor\"))\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-bgcolor>\n    fn SetBgColor(&self, value: DOMString, can_gc: CanGc) {\n        self.set_body_attribute(&local_name!(\"bgcolor\"), value, can_gc)\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-fgcolor>\n    fn FgColor(&self) -> DOMString {\n        self.get_body_attribute(&local_name!(\"text\"))\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-fgcolor>\n    fn SetFgColor(&self, value: DOMString, can_gc: CanGc) {\n        self.set_body_attribute(&local_name!(\"text\"), value, can_gc)\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-tree-accessors:dom-document-nameditem-filter>\n    fn NamedGetter(&self, name: DOMString, can_gc: CanGc) -> Option<NamedPropertyValue> {\n        if name.is_empty() {\n            return None;\n        }\n        let name = Atom::from(name);\n\n        // Step 1. Let elements be the list of named elements with the name name that are in a document tree\n        // with the Document as their root.\n        let elements_with_name = self.get_elements_with_name(&name);\n        let name_iter = elements_with_name\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|elem| is_named_element_with_name_attribute(elem));\n        let elements_with_id = self.get_elements_with_id(&name);\n        let id_iter = elements_with_id\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|elem| is_named_element_with_id_attribute(elem));\n        let mut elements = name_iter.chain(id_iter);\n\n        // Step 2. If elements has only one element, and that element is an iframe element,\n        // and that iframe element's content navigable is not null, then return the active\n        // WindowProxy of the element's content navigable.\n\n        // NOTE: We have to check if all remaining elements are equal to the first, since\n        // the same element may appear in both lists.\n        let first = elements.next()?;\n        if elements.all(|other| first == other) {\n            if let Some(nested_window_proxy) = first\n                .downcast::<HTMLIFrameElement>()\n                .and_then(|iframe| iframe.GetContentWindow())\n            {\n                return Some(NamedPropertyValue::WindowProxy(nested_window_proxy));\n            }\n\n            // Step 3. Otherwise, if elements has only one element, return that element.\n            return Some(NamedPropertyValue::Element(DomRoot::from_ref(first)));\n        }\n\n        // Step 4. Otherwise, return an HTMLCollection rooted at the Document node,\n        // whose filter matches only named elements with the name name.\n        #[derive(JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf)]\n        struct DocumentNamedGetter {\n            #[no_trace]\n            name: Atom,\n        }\n        impl CollectionFilter for DocumentNamedGetter {\n            fn filter(&self, elem: &Element, _root: &Node) -> bool {\n                let type_ = match elem.upcast::<Node>().type_id() {\n                    NodeTypeId::Element(ElementTypeId::HTMLElement(type_)) => type_,\n                    _ => return false,\n                };\n                match type_ {\n                    HTMLElementTypeId::HTMLFormElement | HTMLElementTypeId::HTMLIFrameElement => {\n                        elem.get_name().as_ref() == Some(&self.name)\n                    },\n                    HTMLElementTypeId::HTMLImageElement => elem.get_name().is_some_and(|name| {\n                        name == *self.name ||\n                            !name.is_empty() && elem.get_id().as_ref() == Some(&self.name)\n                    }),\n                    // TODO handle <embed> and <object>; these depend on whether the element is\n                    // “exposed”, a concept that doesn’t fully make sense until embed/object\n                    // behaviour is actually implemented\n                    _ => false,\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        let collection = HTMLCollection::create(\n            self.window(),\n            self.upcast(),\n            Box::new(DocumentNamedGetter { name }),\n            can_gc,\n        );\n        Some(NamedPropertyValue::HTMLCollection(collection))\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-tree-accessors:supported-property-names>\n    fn SupportedPropertyNames(&self) -> Vec<DOMString> {\n        let mut names_with_first_named_element_map: HashMap<&Atom, &Element> = HashMap::new();\n\n        let name_map = self.name_map.borrow();\n        for (name, elements) in &(name_map).0 {\n            if name.is_empty() {\n                continue;\n            }\n            let mut name_iter = elements\n                .iter()\n                .filter(|elem| is_named_element_with_name_attribute(elem));\n            if let Some(first) = name_iter.next() {\n                names_with_first_named_element_map.insert(name, first);\n            }\n        }\n        let id_map = self.id_map.borrow();\n        for (id, elements) in &(id_map).0 {\n            if id.is_empty() {\n                continue;\n            }\n            let mut id_iter = elements\n                .iter()\n                .filter(|elem| is_named_element_with_id_attribute(elem));\n            if let Some(first) = id_iter.next() {\n                match names_with_first_named_element_map.entry(id) {\n                    Vacant(entry) => drop(entry.insert(first)),\n                    Occupied(mut entry) => {\n                        if first.upcast::<Node>().is_before(entry.get().upcast()) {\n                            *entry.get_mut() = first;\n                        }\n                    },\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        let mut names_with_first_named_element_vec: Vec<(&Atom, &Element)> =\n            names_with_first_named_element_map\n                .iter()\n                .map(|(k, v)| (*k, *v))\n                .collect();\n        names_with_first_named_element_vec.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| {\n            if a.1 == b.1 {\n                // This can happen if an img has an id different from its name,\n                // spec does not say which string to put first.\n                a.0.cmp(b.0)\n            } else if a.1.upcast::<Node>().is_before(b.1.upcast::<Node>()) {\n                Ordering::Less\n            } else {\n                Ordering::Greater\n            }\n        });\n\n        names_with_first_named_element_vec\n            .iter()\n            .map(|(k, _v)| DOMString::from(&***k))\n            .collect()\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-clear>\n    fn Clear(&self) {\n        // This method intentionally does nothing\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-captureevents>\n    fn CaptureEvents(&self) {\n        // This method intentionally does nothing\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-releaseevents>\n    fn ReleaseEvents(&self) {\n        // This method intentionally does nothing\n    }\n\n    // https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#globaleventhandlers\n    global_event_handlers!();\n\n    // https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#handler-onreadystatechange\n    event_handler!(\n        readystatechange,\n        GetOnreadystatechange,\n        SetOnreadystatechange\n    );\n\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#dom-document-elementfrompoint>\n    fn ElementFromPoint(&self, x: Finite<f64>, y: Finite<f64>) -> Option<DomRoot<Element>> {\n        self.document_or_shadow_root.element_from_point(\n            x,\n            y,\n            self.GetDocumentElement(),\n            self.has_browsing_context,\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#dom-document-elementsfrompoint>\n    fn ElementsFromPoint(&self, x: Finite<f64>, y: Finite<f64>) -> Vec<DomRoot<Element>> {\n        self.document_or_shadow_root.elements_from_point(\n            x,\n            y,\n            self.GetDocumentElement(),\n            self.has_browsing_context,\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#dom-document-scrollingelement>\n    fn GetScrollingElement(&self) -> Option<DomRoot<Element>> {\n        // Step 1. If the Document is in quirks mode, follow these steps:\n        if self.quirks_mode() == QuirksMode::Quirks {\n            // Step 1.1. If the body element exists,\n            if let Some(ref body) = self.GetBody() {\n                let e = body.upcast::<Element>();\n                // and it is not potentially scrollable, return the body element and abort these steps.\n                // For this purpose, a value of overflow:clip on the body element’s parent element\n                // must be treated as overflow:hidden.\n                if !e.is_potentially_scrollable_body_for_scrolling_element() {\n                    return Some(DomRoot::from_ref(e));\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Step 1.2. Return null and abort these steps.\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        // Step 2. If there is a root element, return the root element and abort these steps.\n        // Step 3. Return null.\n        self.GetDocumentElement()\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-open>\n    fn Open(\n        &self,\n        _unused1: Option<DOMString>,\n        _unused2: Option<DOMString>,\n        can_gc: CanGc,\n    ) -> Fallible<DomRoot<Document>> {\n        // Step 1\n        if !self.is_html_document() {\n            return Err(Error::InvalidState(None));\n        }\n\n        // Step 2\n        if self.throw_on_dynamic_markup_insertion_counter.get() > 0 {\n            return Err(Error::InvalidState(None));\n        }\n\n        // Step 3\n        let entry_responsible_document = GlobalScope::entry().as_window().Document();\n\n        // Step 4\n        if !self.origin.same_origin(&entry_responsible_document.origin) {\n            return Err(Error::Security(None));\n        }\n\n        // Step 5\n        if self\n            .get_current_parser()\n            .is_some_and(|parser| parser.is_active())\n        {\n            return Ok(DomRoot::from_ref(self));\n        }\n\n        // Step 6\n        if self.is_prompting_or_unloading() {\n            return Ok(DomRoot::from_ref(self));\n        }\n\n        // Step 7\n        if self.active_parser_was_aborted.get() {\n            return Ok(DomRoot::from_ref(self));\n        }\n\n        // TODO: prompt to unload.\n        // TODO: set unload_event_start and unload_event_end\n\n        self.window().set_navigation_start();\n\n        // Step 8\n        // TODO: https://github.com/servo/servo/issues/21937\n        if self.has_browsing_context() {\n            // spec says \"stop document loading\",\n            // which is a process that does more than just abort\n            self.abort(can_gc);\n        }\n\n        // Step 9\n        for node in self\n            .upcast::<Node>()\n            .traverse_preorder(ShadowIncluding::Yes)\n        {\n            node.upcast::<EventTarget>().remove_all_listeners();\n        }\n\n        // Step 10\n        if self.window.Document() == DomRoot::from_ref(self) {\n            self.window.upcast::<EventTarget>().remove_all_listeners();\n        }\n\n        // Step 11. Replace all with null within document.\n        Node::replace_all(None, self.upcast::<Node>(), can_gc);\n\n        // Specs and tests are in a state of flux about whether\n        // we want to clear the selection when we remove the contents;\n        // WPT selection/Document-open.html wants us to not clear it\n        // as of Feb 1 2020\n\n        // Step 12. If document is fully active, then:\n        if self.is_fully_active() {\n            // Step 12.1. Let newURL be a copy of entryDocument's URL.\n            let mut new_url = entry_responsible_document.url();\n\n            // Step 12.2. If entryDocument is not document, then set newURL's fragment to null.\n            if entry_responsible_document != DomRoot::from_ref(self) {\n                new_url.set_fragment(None);\n            }\n\n            // Step 12.3. Run the URL and history update steps with document and newURL.\n            // TODO: https://github.com/servo/servo/issues/21939\n            self.set_url(new_url);\n        }\n\n        // Step 13. Set document's is initial about:blank to false.\n        self.is_initial_about_blank.set(false);\n\n        // Step 14. If document's iframe load in progress flag is set, then set document's mute\n        // iframe load flag.\n        // TODO: https://github.com/servo/servo/issues/21938\n\n        // Step 15: Set document to no-quirks mode.\n        self.set_quirks_mode(QuirksMode::NoQuirks);\n\n        // Step 16. Create a new HTML parser and associate it with document. This is a\n        // script-created parser (meaning that it can be closed by the document.open() and\n        // document.close() methods, and that the tokenizer will wait for an explicit call to\n        // document.close() before emitting an end-of-file token). The encoding confidence is\n        // irrelevant.\n        let resource_threads = self.window.as_global_scope().resource_threads().clone();\n        *self.loader.borrow_mut() =\n            DocumentLoader::new_with_threads(resource_threads, Some(self.url()));\n        ServoParser::parse_html_script_input(self, self.url());\n\n        // Step 17. Set the insertion point to point at just before the end of the input stream\n        // (which at this point will be empty).\n        // Handled when creating the parser in step 16\n\n        // Step 18. Update the current document readiness of document to \"loading\".\n        self.ready_state.set(DocumentReadyState::Loading);\n\n        // Step 19. Return document.\n        Ok(DomRoot::from_ref(self))\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-open-window>\n    fn Open_(\n        &self,\n        url: USVString,\n        target: DOMString,\n        features: DOMString,\n        can_gc: CanGc,\n    ) -> Fallible<Option<DomRoot<WindowProxy>>> {\n        self.browsing_context()\n            .ok_or(Error::InvalidAccess(None))?\n            .open(url, target, features, can_gc)\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-write>\n    fn Write(&self, text: Vec<TrustedHTMLOrString>, can_gc: CanGc) -> ErrorResult {\n        // The document.write(...text) method steps are to run the document write steps\n        // with this, text, false, and \"Document write\".\n        self.write(text, false, \"Document\", \"write\", can_gc)\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-writeln>\n    fn Writeln(&self, text: Vec<TrustedHTMLOrString>, can_gc: CanGc) -> ErrorResult {\n        // The document.writeln(...text) method steps are to run the document write steps\n        // with this, text, true, and \"Document writeln\".\n        self.write(text, true, \"Document\", \"writeln\", can_gc)\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-close>\n    fn Close(&self, can_gc: CanGc) -> ErrorResult {\n        if !self.is_html_document() {\n            // Step 1.\n            return Err(Error::InvalidState(None));\n        }\n\n        // Step 2.\n        if self.throw_on_dynamic_markup_insertion_counter.get() > 0 {\n            return Err(Error::InvalidState(None));\n        }\n\n        let parser = match self.get_current_parser() {\n            Some(ref parser) if parser.is_script_created() => DomRoot::from_ref(&**parser),\n            _ => {\n                // Step 3.\n                return Ok(());\n            },\n        };\n\n        // Step 4-6.\n        parser.close(can_gc);\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    // https://fullscreen.spec.whatwg.org/#handler-document-onfullscreenerror\n    event_handler!(fullscreenerror, GetOnfullscreenchange,\n        SetOnfullscreenchange\n    );\n\n    /// <https://fullscreen.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-document-fullscreenenabled>\n    fn FullscreenEnabled(&self) -> bool {\n The script thread is the thread that owns the DOM in memory, runs JavaScript, and triggers\n//! layout. It's in charge of processing events for all same-origin pages in a frame\n//! tree, and manages the entire lifetime of pages in the frame tree from initial request to\n//! teardown.\n//!\n//! Page loads follow a two-step process. When a request for a new page load is received, the\n//! network request is initiated and the relevant data pertaining to the new page is stashed.\n//! While the non-blocking request is ongoing, the script thread is free to process further events,\n//! noting when they pertain to ongoing loads (such as resizes/viewport adjustments). When the\n//! initial response is received for an ongoing load, the second phase starts - the frame tree\n//! entry is created, along with the Window and Document objects, and the appropriate parser\n//! takes over the response body. webdriver_handlers};\n\nthread_local!(static SCRIPT_THREAD_ROOT: Cell<Option<*const ScriptThread>> = const { Cell::new(None) });\n\nfn with_optional_script_thread<R>(f: impl FnOnce(Option<&ScriptThread>) -> R) -> R {\n    SCRIPT_THREAD_ROOT.with(|root| {\n        f(root\n            .get()\n            .and_then(|script_thread| unsafe { script_thread.as_ref() }))\n    })\n}\n\npub(crate) fn with_script_thread<R: Default>(f: impl FnOnce(&ScriptThread) -> R) -> R {\n    with_optional_script_thread(|script_thread| script_thread.map(f).unwrap_or_default())\n}\n\n/// # Safety\n///\n/// The `JSTracer` argument must point to a valid `JSTracer` in memory. In addition,\n/// implementors of this method must ensure that all active objects are properly traced\n/// or else the garbage collector may end up collecting objects that are still reachable.\npub(crate) unsafe fn trace_thread(tr: *mut JSTracer) {\n    with_script_thread(|script_thread| {\n        trace!(\"tracing fields of ScriptThread\");\n        unsafe { script_thread.trace(tr) };\n    })\n}\n\n// We borrow the incomplete parser contexts mutably during parsing,\n// which is fine except that parsing can trigger evaluation,\n// which can trigger GC, and so we can end up tracing the script\n// thread during parsing. For this reason, we don't trace the\n// incomplete parser contexts during GC.\npub(crate) struct IncompleteParserContexts(RefCell<Vec<(PipelineId, ParserContext)>>);\n\nunsafe_no_jsmanaged_fields!(TaskQueue<MainThreadScriptMsg>);\n\ntype NodeIdSet = HashSet<String>;\n\n/// A simple guard structure that restore the user interacting state when dropped\n#[derive(Default)]\npub(crate) struct ScriptUserInteractingGuard {\n    was_interacting: bool,\n    user_interaction_cell: Rc<Cell<bool>>,\n}\n\nimpl ScriptUserInteractingGuard {\n    fn new(user_interaction_cell: Rc<Cell<bool>>) -> Self {\n        let was_interacting = user_interaction_cell.get();\n        user_interaction_cell.set(true);\n        Self {\n            was_interacting,\n            user_interaction_cell,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Drop for ScriptUserInteractingGuard {\n    fn drop(&mut self) {\n        self.user_interaction_cell.set(self.was_interacting)\n    }\n}\n\n/// This is the `ScriptThread`'s version of [`UserContents`] with the difference that user\n/// stylesheets are represented as parsed `DocumentStyleSheet`s instead of simple source strings.\nstruct ScriptThreadUserContents {\n    user_scripts: Rc<Vec<UserScript>>,\n    user_stylesheets: Rc<Vec<DocumentStyleSheet>>,\n}\n\nimpl From<UserContents> for ScriptThreadUserContents {\n    fn from(user_contents: UserContents) -> Self {\n        let shared_lock = &GLOBAL_STYLE_DATA.shared_lock;\n        let user_stylesheets = user_contents\n            .stylesheets\n            .iter()\n            .map(|user_stylesheet| {\n                DocumentStyleSheet(ServoArc::new(Stylesheet::from_str(\n                    user_stylesheet.source(),\n                    user_stylesheet.url().into(),\n                    Origin::User,\n                    ServoArc::new(shared_lock.wrap(MediaList::empty())),\n                    shared_lock.clone(),\n                    None,\n                    Some(&RustLogReporter),\n                    QuirksMode::NoQuirks,\n                    AllowImportRules::Yes,\n                )))\n            })\n            .collect();\n        Self {\n            user_scripts: Rc::new(user_contents.scripts),\n            user_stylesheets: Rc::new(user_stylesheets),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(JSTraceable)]\n// ScriptThread instances are rooted on creation, so this is okay\n#[cfg_attr(crown, expect(crown::unrooted_must_root))]\npub struct ScriptThread {\n    /// A reference to the currently operating `ScriptThread`. This should always be\n    /// upgradable to an `Rc` as long as the `ScriptThread` is running.\n    #[no_trace]\n    this: Weak<ScriptThread>,\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#last-render-opportunity-time>\n    last_render_opportunity_time: Cell<Option<Instant>>,\n\n    /// The documents for pipelines managed by this thread\n    documents: DomRefCell<DocumentCollection>,\n    /// The window proxies known by this thread\n    window_proxies: Rc<ScriptWindowProxies>,\n    /// A list of data pertaining to loads that have not yet received a network response\n    incomplete_loads: DomRefCell<Vec<InProgressLoad>>,\n    /// A vector containing parser contexts which have not yet been fully processed\n    incomplete_parser_contexts: IncompleteParserContexts,\n    /// An [`ImageCacheFactory`] to use for creating [`ImageCache`]s for all of the\n    /// child `Pipeline`s.\n    #[no_trace]\n    image_cache_factory: Arc<dyn ImageCacheFactory>,\n\n    /// A [`ScriptThreadReceivers`] holding all of the incoming `Receiver`s for messages\n    /// to this [`ScriptThread`].\n    receivers: ScriptThreadReceivers,\n\n    /// A [`ScriptThreadSenders`] that holds all outgoing sending channels necessary to communicate\n    /// to other parts of Servo.\n    senders: ScriptThreadSenders,\n\n    /// A handle to the resource thread. This is an `Arc` to avoid running out of file descriptors if\n    /// there are many iframes.\n    #[no_trace]\n    resource_threads: ResourceThreads,\n\n    #[no_trace]\n    storage_threads: StorageThreads,\n\n    /// A queue of tasks to be executed in this script-thread.\n    task_queue: TaskQueue<MainThreadScriptMsg>,\n\n    /// The dedicated means of communication with the background-hang-monitor for this script-thread.\n    #[no_trace]\n    background_hang_monitor: Box<dyn BackgroundHangMonitor>,\n    /// A flag set to `true` by the BHM on exit, and checked from within the interrupt handler.\n    closing: Arc<AtomicBool>,\n\n    /// A [`TimerScheduler`] used to schedule timers for this [`ScriptThread`]. Timers are handled\n    /// in the [`ScriptThread`] event loop.\n    #[no_trace]\n    timer_scheduler: RefCell<TimerScheduler>,\n\n    /// A proxy to the `SystemFontService` to use for accessing system font lists.\n    #[no_trace]\n    system_font_service: Arc<SystemFontServiceProxy>,\n\n    /// The JavaScript runtime.\n    js_runtime: Rc<Runtime>,\n\n    /// List of pipelines that have been owned and closed by this script thread.\n    #[no_trace]\n    closed_pipelines: DomRefCell<FxHashSet<PipelineId>>,\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#microtask-queue>\n    microtask_queue: Rc<MicrotaskQueue>,\n\n    mutation_observers: Rc<ScriptMutationObservers>,\n\n    /// A handle to the WebGL thread\n    #[no_trace]\n    webgl_chan: Option<WebGLPipeline>,\n\n    /// The WebXR device registry\n    #[no_trace]\n    #[cfg(feature = \"webxr\")]\n    webxr_registry: Option<webxr_api::Registry>,\n\n    /// The worklet thread pool\n    worklet_thread_pool: DomRefCell<Option<Rc<WorkletThreadPool>>>,\n\n    /// A list of pipelines containing documents that finished loading all their blocking\n    /// resources during a turn of the event loop.\n    docs_with_no_blocking_loads: DomRefCell<FxHashSet<Dom<Document>>>,\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#custom-element-reactions-stack>\n    custom_element_reaction_stack: Rc<CustomElementReactionStack>,\n\n    /// Cross-process access to `Paint`'s API.\n    #[no_trace]\n    paint_api: CrossProcessPaintApi,\n\n    /// Periodically print out on which events script threads spend their processing time.\n    profile_script_events: bool,\n\n    /// Print Progressive Web Metrics to console.\n    print_pwm: bool,\n\n    /// Unminify Javascript.\n    unminify_js: bool,\n\n    /// Directory with stored unminified scripts\n    local_script_source: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Unminify Css.\n    unminify_css: bool,\n\n    /// A map from [`UserContentManagerId`] to its [`UserContents`]. This is initialized\n    /// with a copy of the map in constellation (via the `InitialScriptState`). After that,\n    /// the constellation forwards any mutations to this `ScriptThread` using messages.\n    #[no_trace]\n    user_contents_for_manager_id:\n        RefCell<FxHashMap<UserContentManagerId, ScriptThreadUserContents>>,\n\n    /// Application window's GL Context for Media player\n    #[no_trace]\n    player_context: WindowGLContext,\n\n    /// A map from pipelines to all owned nodes ever created in this script thread\n    #[no_trace]\n    pipeline_to_node_ids: DomRefCell<FxHashMap<PipelineId, NodeIdSet>>,\n\n    /// Code is running as a consequence of a user interaction\n    is_user_interacting: Rc<Cell<bool>>,\n\n    /// Identity manager for WebGPU resources\n    #[no_trace]\n    #[cfg(feature = \"webgpu\")]\n    gpu_id_hub: Arc<IdentityHub>,\n\n    /// A factory for making new layouts. This allows layout to depend on script.\n    #[no_trace]\n    layout_factory: Arc<dyn LayoutFactory>,\n\n    /// The [`TimerId`] of a ScriptThread-scheduled \"update the rendering\" call, if any.\n    /// The ScriptThread schedules calls to \"update the rendering,\" but the renderer can\n    /// also do this when animating. Renderer-based calls always take precedence.\n    #[no_trace]\n    scheduled_update_the_rendering: RefCell<Option<TimerId>>,\n\n    /// Whether an animation tick or ScriptThread-triggered rendering update is pending. This might\n    /// either be because the Servo renderer is managing animations and the [`ScriptThread`] has\n    /// received a [`ScriptThreadMessage::TickAllAnimations`] message, because the [`ScriptThread`]\n    /// itself is managing animations the timer fired triggering a [`ScriptThread`]-based\n    /// animation tick, or if there are no animations running and the [`ScriptThread`] has noticed a\n    /// change that requires a rendering update.\n    needs_rendering_update: Arc<AtomicBool>,\n\n    debugger_global: Dom<DebuggerGlobalScope>,\n\n    /// A list of URLs that can access privileged internal APIs.\n    #[no_trace]\n    privileged_urls: Vec<ServoUrl>,\n}\n\nstruct BHMExitSignal {\n    closing: Arc<AtomicBool>,\n    js_context: ThreadSafeJSContext,\n}\n\nimpl BackgroundHangMonitorExitSignal for BHMExitSignal {\n    fn signal_to_exit(&self) {\n        self.closing.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);\n        self.js_context.request_interrupt_callback();\n    }\n}\n\n#[expect(unsafe_code)]\nunsafe extern \"C\" fn interrupt_callback(_cx: *mut UnsafeJSContext) -> bool {\n    let res = ScriptThread::can_continue_running();\n    if !res {\n        ScriptThread::prepare_for_shutdown();\n    }\n    res\n}\n\n/// In the event of thread panic, all data on the stack runs its destructor. However, there\n/// are no reachable, owning pointers to the DOM memory, so it never gets freed by default\n/// when the script thread fails. The ScriptMemoryFailsafe uses the destructor bomb pattern\n/// to forcibly tear down the JS realms for pages associated with the failing ScriptThread.\nstruct ScriptMemoryFailsafe<'a> {\n    owner: Option<&'a ScriptThread>,\n}\n\nimpl<'a> ScriptMemoryFailsafe<'a> {\n    fn neuter(&mut self) {\n        self.owner = None;\n    }\n\n    fn new(owner: &'a ScriptThread) -> ScriptMemoryFailsafe<'a> {\n        ScriptMemoryFailsafe { owner: Some(owner) }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Drop for ScriptMemoryFailsafe<'_> {\n    fn drop(&mut self) {\n        if let Some(owner) = self.owner {\n            for (_, document) in owner.documents.borrow().iter() {\n                document.window().clear_js_runtime_for_script_deallocation();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl ScriptThreadFactory for ScriptThread {\n    fn create(\n        state: InitialScriptState,\n        layout_factory: Arc<dyn LayoutFactory>,\n        image_cache_factory: Arc<dyn ImageCacheFactory>,\n        background_hang_monitor_register: Box<dyn BackgroundHangMonitorRegister>,\n    ) -> JoinHandle<()> {\n        // Setup pipeline-namespace-installing for all threads in this process.\n        // Idempotent in single-process mode.\n        PipelineNamespace::set_installer_sender(state.namespace_request_sender.clone());\n\n        let script_thread_id = state.id;\n        thread::Builder::new()\n            .name(format!(\"Script#{script_thread_id}\"))\n            .spawn(move || {\n                thread_state::initialize(ThreadState::SCRIPT | ThreadState::LAYOUT);\n                PipelineNamespace::install(state.pipeline_namespace_id);\n                ScriptEventLoopId::install(state.id);\n                let memory_profiler_sender = state.memory_profiler_sender.clone();\n                let reporter_name = format!(\"script-reporter-{script_thread_id:?}\");\n                let (script_thread, mut cx) = ScriptThread::new(\n                    state,\n                    layout_factory,\n                    image_cache_factory,\n                    background_hang_monitor_register,\n                );\n                SCRIPT_THREAD_ROOT.with(|root| {\n                    root.set(Some(Rc::as_ptr(&script_thread)));\n                });\n                let mut failsafe = ScriptMemoryFailsafe::new(&script_thread);\n\n                memory_profiler_sender.run_with_memory_reporting(\n                    || script_thread.start(&mut cx),\n                    reporter_name,\n                    ScriptEventLoopSender::MainThread(script_thread.senders.self_sender.clone()),\n                    CommonScriptMsg::CollectReports,\n                );\n\n                // This must always be the very last operation performed before the thread completes\n                failsafe.neuter();\n            })\n            .expect(\"Thread spawning failed\")\n    }\n}\n\nimpl ScriptThread {\n    pub(crate) fn runtime_handle() -> ParentRuntime {\n        with_optional_script_thread(|script_thread| {\n            script_thread.unwrap().js_runtime.prepare_for_new_child()\n        })\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn can_continue_running() -> bool {\n        with_script_thread(|script_thread| script_thread.can_continue_running_inner())\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn prepare_for_shutdown() {\n        with_script_thread(|script_thread| {\n            script_thread.prepare_for_shutdown_inner();\n        })\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn mutation_observers() -> Rc<ScriptMutationObservers> {\n        with_script_thread(|script_thread| script_thread.mutation_observers.clone())\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn microtask_queue() -> Rc<MicrotaskQueue> {\n        with_script_thread(|script_thread| script_thread.microtask_queue.clone())\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn mark_document_with_no_blocked_loads(doc: &Document) {\n        with_script_thread(|script_thread| {\n            script_thread\n                .docs_with_no_blocking_loads\n                .borrow_mut()\n                .insert(Dom::from_ref(doc));\n        })\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn page_headers_available(\n        webview_id: WebViewId,\n        pipeline_id:
